raw.objective: “to develop a population pharmacokinetic model of topiramate to assist dosage adjustments in individual patients”
raw.covariates: Tested: Age, Gender, WT, BSA, AST, ALT, CLcr, DTD, BZD, CBZ, RIS, VPA Inlcuded: CBZ and Age on CL/F and WT on V/F
raw.observations: Topiramate PK was described by a 1-compartment model. Volume of distribution of topiramate was 0.518 l/kg. For a typical patient (30 years) oral clearance was estimated at 1.47 l/h, with interindividual variability of 39.2%. Clearance was 70% higher in patients co-treated with carbamazepine and was found to increase with patient age.
raw.structure: 1-compartment with first order absorption and elimination
raw.equations: CL/F = theta1*(theta2**CBZ)*((Age/30)**theta3)*exp(eta1) V/F = theta4*WT Ka=2 Cobs = Cipred*(1+eps1)
raw.parameter_values: Theta1 = 1.47 Theta2 = 1.70 Theta3 = 0.421 Theta4 = 0.518 Omega1 = 0.143 Sigma1 = 0.019

extraction_target.title: A nonlinear mixed effects modelling analysis of topiramate pharmacokinetics in patients with epilepsy.

structural_model.description: 1-compartment with first order absorption and elimination

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: CL/F = theta1*(theta2**CBZ)*((Age/30)**theta3)*exp(eta1) V/F = theta4*WT Ka=2 Cobs = Cipred*(1+eps1)
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to develop a population pharmacokinetic model of topiramate to assist dosage adjustments in individual patients”

tested: VPA Inlcuded: CBZ and Age on CL/F and WT on V/F
covariates.raw: Tested: Age, Gender, WT, BSA, AST, ALT, CLcr, DTD, BZD, CBZ, RIS, VPA Inlcuded: CBZ and Age on CL/F and WT on V/F

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
